channel.title: Home on Ezra Natanael
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Ezra Natanael

item.title: How to Focus on One Programming Language
item.description: In the world of programming, it’s easy to feel overwhelmed by choices. Every few months, a new language makes headlines, promising to be faster, simpler, or better than everything else. If you’re like me, you’ve probably jumped on a few of these bandwagons, hoping to find the perfect language. I spent years dabbling in different languages - Java, Python, Ruby, C++, JavaScript - convinced that each one would be the key to unlocking my potential. But looking back, I realize I wasted so much time learning languages I barely touched again.

item.title: Why Code Conventions Matter
item.description:
Introduction

In my learning journey over the past 2 years, after tackling various projects and internships, one lesson I’ve carried with me is this: consistency in code matters. Whether I was building scalable RESTful APIs, integrating third-party services, or working with distributed systems, I realized that adhering to a code convention not only made my projects clean and readable but also improved collaboration with teammates.

Early on, I didn’t pay much attention to coding style - what mattered to me was making it “work.” But as projects grew larger, bugs harder to track, and teammates more involved, I saw firsthand how inconsistent code slows you down. Naming confusion, messy formatting, or inconsistent structures caused hours of frustration.

item.title: Bangkit Has Come to an End
item.description:
My nearly four-month journey in Bangkit has finally come to an end. It all started when I was 19 years old and saw my senior join the Bangkit program. At first, I had no idea what it was, but after looking it up, it instantly caught my attention.

Like I mentioned in my previous article, Bangkit is a program by major companies like Google and Tokopedia that provides training in specific topics. At the time of writing this, there are three learning paths: Cloud Computing, which I chose, Mobile Development, and Machine Learning.

item.title: Understanding and Preventing Nil Pointer Error in Go
item.description:
Introduction

If you’re coding in Go, there’s one error that’s bound to trip you up at some point: the nil pointer error. It can throw you off, whether you’re a beginner or have some experience. But no need to stress - let me break it down for you.

Think of your computer’s memory as a big storage unit with lots of compartments, each with its own unique address. A pointer is like a sticky note with one of those addresses written on it, so you can easily find that compartment later.

item.title: Surviving Bangkit
item.description:
If someone asked me to sum up my Bangkit experience in one word, I’d say:

Resilience (fancy way of saying “not giving up when things get crazy”)

Picture this: Google teamed up with Indonesia’s tech giants (you know, Gojek, Tokopedia, Traveloka) and our Ministry of Education to create this intense tech bootcamp. They offer three flavors: Cloud Computing, Machine Learning, and Mobile Development.

Not gonna lie - this program isn’t a walk in the park. Between drowning in assignments, jumping from one meeting to another, sitting through Instructor-Led Training (ILT), and trying to keep up with college… let’s just say coffee became my best friend.

item.title: A Glimpse Into My Life
item.description:
Hey! I’m Ezra, and I spend my days building cool stuff as a software engineer here in Indonesia. While I’m pretty much glued to my keyboard writing code, there’s definitely more to me than just staring at screens all day.

This website? It’s basically my little corner of the internet where I dump my thoughts, share what I’m learning, and hopefully help out other devs along the way. I got tired of the whole polished, perfect-looking tech persona thing, so I decided to just be real here.

item.title: Library
item.description:
Here’s my stash of favorite tech books and resources that I keep coming back to.

These have been game-changers for me - check them out!

  • Clean Architecture: A Craftsman’s Guide to Software Structure and Design

This book is packed with practical tips to make your software easier to build and maintain over the long run. Uncle Bob brings decades of experience to the table, giving you straightforward advice to make the right design choices that can make or break a project.
